name: Grave Detail
desc: Summons digger adds throughout the fight; every add death feeds Bursting.

name: Preserving Fume
desc: A party-wide damage-over-time cast that must be interrupted.

name: Pooling Sorrow
desc: Avoidable pools under characters; standing in them is avoidable damage.

name: Final Coronation
desc: A timed damage-spike phase with burn windows between.

name: Antiphon
desc: A party-wide liturgical nuke sung every ~12s; uninterrupted, each peal lands harder than the last until the party craters. Kick it, or bring a real interrupter.

name: Drowning Psalm
desc: The fused chorus sings the same stacking party-nuke on a TIGHTER telegraph — less time to kick, so a real interrupter earns its slot.

name: Toll the Hours
desc: The one breather: a timed party-wide spike with burn windows between — rewards the points banked into Cooldowns.

name: Final Peal
desc: The capstone cast: each unanswered peal stacks harder, and one too many into soft-enrage ends the run.

name: Stillwater Pool
desc: On each toll a pool floods under one celebrant — an avoidable single-target spike a rolling HoT can't pre-load.

name: Evensong
desc: On a tight cadence the toll falls on whoever is already flagging — an instant burst heal or a pre-applied absorb saves them; a rolling HoT recovers too slowly.

name: Vespers Toll
desc: A heavier toll that hunts the weakest in the party; top them fast or lose them between beats.

name: The Final Hour
desc: The loudest toll on the tightest cadence — the flagging one drops every beat unless burst-healed or pre-shielded.

name: Mire Swell
desc: The fen swells; the rot rises across the party — a flat, ceaseless tick that rolling HoTs outlast and reactive single-target heals fall behind.

name: Drowning Grip
desc: The water closes over everyone at once; pre-laid HoTs keep five heads above it where burst triage can't reach them all.

name: Rising Tide
desc: The tide comes in across the whole party; only steady, rolling healing outlasts a long enough rise.

name: The Glut
desc: The fen feasts on the whole party at once; Cooldowns blunts the tick, but it's rolling HoTs that decide the ceiling.

name: Grave-Iron Bulwark
desc: Iron-bound and blade-blunting — bring Magic to the grave-iron, or watch your physical dealers chip a wall.

name: Salt Ward
desc: Warding-salt drinks the spell before it lands — bring the blade to the salt-warded, not the bolt.

name: Annealing Pyre
desc: Tempered iron, cooled in the dark; a spike phase that wants Magic dealers to actually move the bar.

name: Heretic Rite
desc: A salt-warded finale: burn the word from the air, and bring the blade since the spell just rolls off.

name: Keep the Hour
desc: The first toll of the gauntlet — a party-wide spike on the hour. Bank Cooldowns for the windows; the clock is the real boss.

name: Leaden Toll
desc: The bell tolls leaden wardens up to ward it — cut a warden down to land a blow on the Carillon, or beat on its ward for nothing.

name: Matins Knell
desc: The bells never stop; pace your majors across the gauntlet or run out before the last peal.

name: Vespers Dark
desc: The dark hour — the spike bites hardest into soft-enrage if you've over-pulled the clock.

name: The Last Peal
desc: Pop everything now or pop nothing and be tolled — the capstone spike under the dying clock.
